headline: Citi Cuts Palantir Price Target to $210: Is the AI Software Multiple Compression Catching Up?
summary: Citi lowered its price target on Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR) stock to $210 from $260 while keeping a Buy rating, citing recent multiple compression in software. Notably, the firm raised its Palantir estimates ahead of the Q1 2026 report, signaling fundamentals remain intact even as valuations across the sector reset. The analyst pointed to continued momentum tied ... Citi Cuts Palantir Price Target to $210: Is the AI Software Multiple Compression Catching Up?

headline: Adobe Semrush Deal Aims To Recast AI Marketing And Search Workflows
summary: Adobe has completed its acquisition of Semrush Holdings, adding AI driven brand visibility and marketing tools to its portfolio. The deal expands Adobe's capabilities in SEO, agentic search optimization and digital discoverability for enterprise customers. The transaction comes alongside broader launches in Adobe's enterprise AI products and partnerships with large marketing groups. Adobe, NasdaqGS:ADBE, is moving to deepen its role in digital marketing at a time when its share price sits...

headline: Alluvium and Adobe Partner to Advance Supply and Demand Intelligence for Health Systems
summary: MCKINNEY, Texas & SAN JOSE, Calif., April 28, 2026--Alluvium, a leader in enterprise access and capacity performance for health systems, today announced a collaboration with Adobe to bring next-level intelligence to healthcare capacity management. headline: Mizuho resets Adobe stock price target for the rest of 2026
summary: Adobe has had a brutal year. The stock is down roughly 31% year to date, trading near $245 and sitting much closer to its 52-week low than its 52-week high. Now one of its last major bulls on Wall Street has changed its mind. The downgrade from Mizuho lands with extra weight because the firm admits ...

headline: Midday Fly By: Verizon reports ‘beat and raise,’ Shell to acquire ARC
summary: The major averages were broadly lower amid stalled peace negotiations between the U.S. and Iran as well as new escalation in the Strait of Hormuz. Investors are also balancing continued strength in corporate earnings and AI-driven optimism with rising geopolitical tension and macro uncertainty.
